assertions.text: The car exited a curve (was coming out of the curve) before it slid off the road.
The car slid off the road (loss of lateral control leading to leaving the roadway).
The car struck (clipped) a utility pole, implying impact with a stationary pole alongside/near the road.
The contact with the utility pole was light and glancing.
The car required only cosmetic repairs after the collision (no structural damage claimed).
There was a single impact with the utility pole (one contact).

verdicts.evidence: The claim that the car "exited a curve" before sliding is a sequence/road-shape assertion not directly recorded by sensors. Telemetry shows steering peaks up to [27.0 deg](T2) at [t=-0.2 s](T1) and steering baseline [4.5 deg median](T2), but road curvature or that the vehicle was "coming out of a curve" cannot be determined from these channels alone, so this is unverifiable.

The claim that the car "slid off the road" (loss of lateral control leaving the roadway) is about location relative to the road surface and path. Sensors show steering activity with a peak of [27.0 deg](T2) at [t=-0.2 s](T1) and a lateral cumulative delta-V final value of [ -6.9 km/h](T2), but the device-frame lateral delta-V and steering do not alone prove the vehicle left the roadway, so this is unverifiable.

The assertion of impact with a utility pole (a stationary roadside pole) is about the struck object. Telemetry records a large longitudinal cumulative delta-V of [ -75.6 km/h](T2) peaking at [t=0.24 s](T2) and small lateral cumulative delta-V peak magnitude [8.8 km/h](T2), but sensors do not identify the object struck (pole vs. other), so this is unverifiable.

The claim that the contact was "light and glancing" is contradicted by the crash magnitude: the cumulative longitudinal delta-V reached [ -75.6 km/h](T2) (final value [ -75.6 km/h](T2)) with peak at [t=0.24 s](T2), which indicates a severe impact inconsistent with a light/glancing contact.

The claim that the car required only cosmetic repairs is contradicted by the recorded large crash pulse: longitudinal delta-V final [ -75.6 km/h](T2) and max abs delta-V [75.6 km/h](T2), which are characteristic of high-severity collisions and make the "only cosmetic" outcome unlikely; telemetry therefore contradicts the assertion.

The assertion of a single impact is supported by the crash-pulse data showing one dominant cumulative delta-V curve reaching a single final value of [ -75.6 km/h](T2) at [t=0.24 s](T2) with no separate secondary peaks reported in delta-V channels, consistent with one impact event.

result.rationale: Multiple assertions about a light, glancing single cosmetic-impact are contradicted by the telemetry which records a large longitudinal delta-V of [ -75.6 km/h](T2), indicating a severe impact; other scene/location claims are unverifiable from sensors alone.

uncited_quantitative_claims.context: condary peaks reported in delta-V channels, consistent with one impact event.

result.time_zero: impact (t=0); pre-crash negative, crash pulse positive

channels.name: delta_v_long
channels.desc: Cumulative longitudinal delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

channels.name: delta_v_lat
channels.desc: Cumulative lateral delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

result.notes: NHTSA CISS EDR data: sparse pre-crash series (~2 Hz, t<0) plus a crash-pulse delta-V curve (t>=0, ~10 ms steps). t=0 is the impact.

delta_v_long.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

delta_v_lat.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse
